theme.title: Quiet Session
theme.summary: Materials (+0.19%) and Consumer Discretionary (-0.00%) led the market today, while Industrials (-0.76%) and Consumer Staples (-0.25%) brought up the rear. Performance didn't cluster cleanly by style — growth averaged -0.06%, defensives -0.12%, and value/cyclicals -0.15% — more a stock-by-stock market than a single broad theme. That shows up at the index level too: the tech-heavy Nasdaq gained +1.30% while the blue-chip Dow lagged at +0.27%. Small caps bucked the broader trend, with the Russell 2000 up +1.22%, outperforming large caps. The VIX rose 1.64% to 16.10, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 0.49% to $4,109.30, crude oil was little changed at $71.98, and bitcoin gained 1.50% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
